summary: QA accepts the M3 LLM JSON client slice. The reusable LLM boundary, explicit OpenAI-compatible adapter configuration, strict JSON extraction, Zod validation, one-repair flow, fake-transport coverage, R2 secret non-exposure regression, README documentation, and required validations all pass.

acceptance_review.llm_client_interface: PASS: LlmClient, typed chat input, token usage, success/failure result unions, and structured error surfaces are defined in services/llm/types.ts.
acceptance_review.openai_compatible_adapter: PASS: OpenAICompatibleClient accepts explicit baseUrl, apiKey, model, and optional fetchImplementation, fails fast on blank required config, and no direct process.env read was found in services/llm.
acceptance_review.safe_json_completion: PASS: safeJsonCompletion parses assistant JSON, validates with the caller Zod schema, attempts one repair completion at most, and returns structured success/failure results without throwing on malformed output.
acceptance_review.json_extraction: PASS: extractJsonText accepts plain top-level JSON and a single whole fenced JSON block while rejecting prose-wrapped JSON.
acceptance_review.tests: PASS: tests cover valid JSON, fenced extraction/prose rejection, parse repair, validation plus repair failure, OpenAI-compatible fake fetch request shape, missing config, and the R2 HTTP error body non-exposure regression.
acceptance_review.readme: PASS: README documents the LLM JSON utility, explicit config boundary, no-secret/no-network fake transport policy, npm test command, and build check.
acceptance_review.scope: PASS: No new real LLM call, environment secret loading, agent implementation, scan orchestration, persistence, API route, UI, auth, cron, deploy, or paid service setup was found in the reviewed LLM slice.

tests_run.result: Passed: Next.js production build completed with non-fatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc warnings.
Passed; command emitted only CRLF normalization warnings for existing modified text files.

decisions: Accepted the R2 security fix because the HTTP error path no longer reads or returns raw provider response bodies and the regression test proves fake secrets/request material are absent from serialized structured errors.
Accepted the strict JSON extraction policy because prose-wrapped output is rejected before JSON.parse, while plain JSON and a single fenced JSON block are supported.
Accepted the scope as limited to the LLM utility boundary plus tests and README documentation; project workflow metadata and handoff files are treated as orchestration context.
risks: Build continues to emit non-fatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings before exiting successfully.
Provider-specific HTTP error body diagnostics are intentionally not surfaced; future server-side logging should remain explicitly redacted if added.
requested_action: Proceed with orchestration after QA acceptance.
